get.description:
Fetches metadata for all registered channels.
Each entry includes schema, metadata, tags, and the number of available rows.
No data rows are returned in this endpoint.
Optionally, filter by a name or tag_key/tag_value pair.

parameters.name: tags

schema.description: Optional tags as comma-separated key:value pairs, e.g., color:red
schema.title: Tags

parameters.description: Optional tags as comma-separated key:value pairs, e.g., color:red

parameters.name: metadata

schema.description: Optional tags as comma-separated key:value pairs, e.g., color:red
schema.title: Metadata

parameters.description: Optional tags as comma-separated key:value pairs, e.g., color:red

schema.title: Response Fetch All Channels Metadata Api V1 Channels Get

get.summary: Fetch metadata for a specific channel
get.description:
Retrieves metadata for the specified `channel_id`, including schema, tags, and metadata.
The response also includes the number of rows stored for that channel
but does not include the row data itself.

get.summary: Fetch stored rows for a specific channel
get.description:
Retrieves all stored time-series rows for the specified `channel_id`.
Filtering, ordering, pagination, and column selection are supported via query parameters.
The response includes both metadata and data rows according to the channel’s schema.

get.summary: Fetch the data as columns for a specific channel
get.description:
Retrieves all stored time-series rows for the specified `channel_id` and present them as columns.
The response includes both metadata and data columns according to the channel’s schema.

post.description: Create a new channel with metadata and schema. Does not add rows, and fails if the channel already exists.

put.description: Upsert a channel: if it exists, updates metadata (schema must match if provided); if it does not exist, creates it with the given schema and metadata.

get.summary: Fetch metadata for a specific channel
get.description:
Retrieves metadata for the specified `channel_id`, including schema, tags, and metadata.
The response also includes the number of rows stored for that channel
but does not include the row data itself.

patch.description: Update only a subset of metadata, tags, or schema for the specified channel.

delete.description: Deletes a channel and its metadata. All data associated with the channel is also removed. Returns the metadata of the deleted channel.

get.summary: Fetch stored rows for a specific channel
get.description:
Retrieves all stored time-series rows for the specified `channel_id`.
Filtering, ordering, pagination, and column selection are supported via query parameters.
The response includes both metadata and data rows according to the channel’s schema.

post.description: Append-only operation: insert new rows for an existing channel. Does not modify metadata or schema.

parameters.name: strict

schema.description: If true, abort on any validation error
schema.title: Strict

parameters.description: If true, abort on any validation error

delete.summary: Delete all rows for a channel
delete.description: Removes all data rows for the specified channel, while keeping its metadata intact.

get.summary: Fetch the data as columns for a specific channel
get.description:
Retrieves all stored time-series rows for the specified `channel_id` and present them as columns.
The response includes both metadata and data columns according to the channel’s schema.

post.description: Creates a new channel by cloning an existing channel's schema and optionally metadata. Does not copy data rows nor name.

get.description: Retrieves every Python-like type that can be used to describe the type of a data column

schema.title: Response Get Column Types Setup V1 Schemas Column Types Get
Response List Users Rbac V1 Users Get

db_to_usr.title: Db To Usr
db_to_usr.description: DB column name mapping -> User-defined column name

ChannelSchema.title: ChannelSchema
ChannelSchema.description:
ChannelSchema is a pure structural definition of a channel's timeseries layout.

Responsibilities:
- Define user-facing column names and their types.
- Provide validation rules for rows.
- Provide deterministic user↔internal column normalization.
- Offer structural comparison (e.g. for migration/versioning).

It is:
- Storage-agnostic (no table name, no SQL, no DB types).
- Immutable (once created, structure does not change).
- Safe to serialize and embed in API payloads (part of ChannelMetadata).

It does NOT:
- Know about channel_id.
- Generate SQL.
- Know about row id or received_at.
- Compare itself to a live database table.

Database materialization is handled by ChannelTimeseries.

strict.title: Strict
strict.description: If true, any validation error aborts the entire request.

InputPayload.title: InputPayload
InputPayload.description:
User-provided payload for a channel.
The 'channel_schema' field is a dict of column_name -> type string.

InputUser.title: InputUser
InputUser.description:
Represents raw input data for user creation.
Validates and sanitizes user-provided data before processing.
May reject invalid data (e.g., weak passwords, invalid emails).

login.title: Login
login.description: Username or email used to login

password.title: Password
password.description: User password (validated against server password policy)

login.title: Login
login.description: Username or email used to login

password.title: Password
password.description: User password (validated against server password policy)

OutputUser.title: OutputUser
OutputUser.description:
Represents validated and processed user data ready for database insertion.
Contains derived fields (e.g., hashed passwords) and metadata.

ResponsePayload.title: ResponsePayload
ResponsePayload.description:
Payload returned in responses to the user.
- issued_at: when the response was generated
- available_rows: number of rows stored for this channel (None if unknown)
